_note: Test prompts for the skill-creator eval loop. Prompts are realistic user asks. Assertions are objectively checkable; they are drafted but not yet run — invoke the skill-creator eval loop to benchmark with-skill vs. baseline.

evals.name: diagnose-build-breaks
evals.prompt: Every time I let Claude Code work on my Python API it says it finished but the build is broken and half the time it touched files I never asked about. It's a FastAPI repo with pytest already set up. How do I stop this from happening?
evals.expected_output: Attributes the symptom to harness layers (no Definition of Done / completion gate = feedback; touching unrelated files = no scope limit). Recommends WIP=1, an executable Definition of Done, a completion gate where the agent can't self-pass, and a routing AGENTS.md with verification commands. Should reference the diagnostic loop rather than suggesting a different model.

assertions.text: Attributes failures to specific harness subsystems (feedback/state/instructions) before proposing fixes
Recommends WIP=1 / one-active-task to address the over-touching of files
Recommends an executable Definition of Done or completion gate (not the agent self-declaring done)
Does NOT primarily recommend switching to a more capable model

evals.name: setup-agents-md-from-scratch
evals.prompt: I want to make my Node/TypeScript monorepo friendly for AI coding agents. There's nothing set up right now beyond a README. Can you set up the instruction and verification scaffolding for me?
evals.expected_output: Produces a routing-style AGENTS.md (50-200 lines: overview, run commands, verification commands, <=15 hard constraints, routing map to topic docs) rather than one giant file. Includes explicit verification commands and a Definition of Done. May add PROGRESS.md / DECISIONS.md / feature list. Runs or references the fresh-session test.

assertions.text: Creates a routing AGENTS.md kept short (not a monolithic 600-line file) that links to topic docs
Includes <=15 short, testable hard constraints rather than a long flat rule list
References the fresh-session test (what is this / how to run / how to verify / where are we)

evals.name: context-lost-across-sessions
evals.prompt: My agent forgets everything between sessions — each new chat re-explores the repo for 20 minutes and sometimes redoes work that was already done or undoes earlier decisions. It's a multi-day project. What's the fix?
evals.expected_output: Identifies this as a State/continuity failure. Recommends the handoff stack (PROGRESS.md for living state, DECISIONS.md for the why, atomic commits), clock-in/clock-out routines, handing off before ~60% context, and a feature list so finished work isn't redone. Emphasizes persisting the why, the state, and the next move in the repo (not chat).

assertions.text: Identifies the failure as a state/continuity (handoff) problem
Recommends PROGRESS.md and DECISIONS.md (state + the 'why') stored in the repo, not chat history
Recommends a feature list or scope surface so completed work is not redone
Mentions clock-in/clock-out or handing off before context runs out
